params.name: Zircon of Jack Hills
params.description:
On May 13, 2001 AD, scientists discovered the oldest zircons in the world.
Zircon of Jack Hills is a remarkable piece of Earth's crust formed at the beginning of the planet's existence, just 120 million years younger than Earth. They hold vital information about the crust's composition during the Hadean Eon, providing insights into the Earth's cooling process, the formation of liquid water, and the evolution of life. Its discovery has advanced our knowledge of the early crust's nature, the hi …
